meaning_english: To solemnly renounce a belief, cause, or claim. Synonyms: Relinquish, Reject, Forswear.
To surprise or impress someone greatly. Synonyms: Astound, Stun, Flabbergast.
To encourage someone to do something more quickly or to keep moving. Synonyms: Nag, Hasten, Prod.
To catch sight of something distant or obscure. Synonyms: Discern, Spot, Espy.
To gradually advance beyond usual or acceptable limits. Synonyms: Intrude, Impinge, Trespass.
To waste time, money, or energy on trifling matters. Synonyms: Squander, Dissipate, Lavish.
To treat a mistake or problem as if it is not important. Synonyms: Conceal, Mask, Veil.
To have a strong desire or longing for something. Synonyms: Yearn, Crave, Covet.
To persuade someone to do something by means of deception or flattery. Synonyms: Entice, Wheedle, Coax.
To abandon or discard something that is no longer wanted. Synonyms: Discard, Dump, Shed.
To begin to work hard or apply oneself seriously. Synonyms: Buckle down, Concentrate.
To be completely honest and frank with someone. Synonyms: Be candid, Open up.
To think about something deeply and for a long time. Synonyms: Ruminate, Ponder, Contemplate.
To discover information by searching, or to defeat someone by a very narrow margin. Synonyms: Detect, Scent.
To call into question or to contradict. Synonyms: Challenge, Dispute, Contest.
To get rid of something by giving it to someone else dishonestly. Synonyms: Foist, Deceive.
To feel or show fear or apprehension. Synonyms: Cower, Recoil, Flinch.
To bring up old, unpleasant facts or memories. Synonyms: Revive, Recall.
To dishonestly take money from an account or organization. Synonyms: Embezzle, Drain.
To treat someone or something without seriousness or respect. Synonyms: Toy with, Dally.
To experience or endure a difficult process or change. Synonyms: Endure, Suffer.
To improve the appearance of something to make it more attractive. Synonyms: Revamp, Refurbish.
To remove people or things from a group until only the best are left. Synonyms: Filter, Sift.
To cross something out or delete a specific detail. Synonyms: Erase, Strike out.
To give up possession of something or to reveal a secret. Synonyms: Relinquish, Surrender.
To focus all your attention on one particular thing. Synonyms: Pinpoint, Target.
To ask many people the same question. Synonyms: Inquire.
To rely on something happening. Synonyms: Count on, Depend.
To refuse to speak or share information. Synonyms: Go silent.
To think about something negative for a long period. Synonyms: Harp on.
